meta_title: For Founders: SaaS pricing to Solve no project ideas (Beginner Case Study)
meta_description: Learn how to SaaS pricing and overcome no project ideas. A Beginner-level Case Study using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Founders: SaaS pricing to Solve no project ideas
content.seo_description: Learn how to master SaaS pricing and overcome the no project ideas hurdle. A beginner-level case study using Antigravity and Claude Code for rapid growth.
content.answer_block: Stop chasing 'perfect' ideas. Instead, build a pricing-first MVP. By defining your revenue model—tiered subscriptions, usage-based, or flat-fee—before writing code, you force market validation. Use Antigravity to scaffold your architecture and Claude Code to iterate on billing logic, ensuring every feature serves a paying customer, not just your ego.
content.intro:
The blank screen is the graveyard of ambition. You have the technical skill to build, but the silence of 'no project ideas' keeps you paralyzed. You aren't suffering from a lack of creativity; you are suffering from a lack of constraints. Without a pricing model to anchor your development, your project is just a hobby, not a business.

True SaaS architecture starts with the checkout flow. When you define how you capture value, the features reveal themselves. This case study strips away the fluff, showing you how to bypass the 'idea' trap by building a revenue-validated engine. We’ll use Antigravity for rapid scaffolding and Claude Code for precision implementation, turning your first hour into a deployable asset.

steps.title: Define Your Revenue Anchor
steps.description: Before a single line of code, document the value proposition. Decide if you are selling time-saved (B2B SaaS) or utility (B2C API).
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a lean SaaS project structure for a subscription-based micro-tool. Include a database schema for user roles and a Stripe integration skeleton.

steps.title: Build the Pricing Tiers
steps.description: Implement a tiered pricing strategy that separates 'Free' users from 'Power' users, creating a clear upgrade path.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a pricing component that displays three tiers: Free, Pro, and Enterprise, with logic to handle feature-gating based on the user's subscription status.

steps.title: Integrate Stripe Checkout
steps.description: Connect your pricing model to real-world transactions. This is the most critical step for business validation.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Setup a Stripe Checkout session handler in a Node.js backend. Include a webhook endpoint for handling subscription success events.

steps.title: Implement Feature Gating
steps.description: Ensure that your code checks for subscription status before executing premium features, enforcing your business rules.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a middleware utility that checks if a user's subscription level permits access to specific API endpoints.

steps.title: Deploy and Iterate
steps.description: Push your MVP to production. Use the feedback loop to see if the pricing structure you chose actually converts users.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Configure a deployment pipeline for Vercel that includes environment variable validation for Stripe API keys.

pro_tips: Always price based on the value provided to the customer's wallet, not the cost of your server resources.
Use a 'hard wall' for your most valuable feature; if users aren't willing to pay for it, your idea isn't worth building.
Automate your churn emails; a proactive cancellation survey is your best source of 'no project ideas' market research.
Start with a single, flat-fee pricing tier to validate interest before adding complexity with subscription models.
Use Claude Code to write unit tests for your checkout logic; payment bugs are the fastest way to kill founder reputation.
content.next_steps: Once your pricing is live, focus on acquiring your first 10 users through direct outreach to validate the product-market fit before scaling your marketing efforts.
content.conversion_block: Don't let another day pass waiting for the 'perfect' idea. Build the infrastructure, set the price, and let the market tell you what to build next. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
